DISCOVERY 610, RESPONDENT JOHN C. OWENS'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER ANSWERS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES: GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. #11: DENIED, THE INTERROGATORY IS OVERBROAD. #12: GRANTED IN PART. PETITIONERS ARE ORDERED TO RESPOND BY IDENTIFYING ANY MEDICAL PROVIDERS WHO ARE NOT IDENTIFIED IN THE MEDICAL RECORDS FROM KAISER SAN FRANCISCO. #13: DENIED, THE INTERROGATORY IS OVERBROAD. THE REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. IF EITHER PARTY WISHES TO CONTEST THE TENTATIVE RULING, NOTICE MUST BE GIVEN PURSUANT TO LOCAL RULE 8.3 WITH A HEARING DATE OF AUGUST 22, 2006 AT 9:00 A.M. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-05-2008 Vacated

Verified Petition To Determine Validity Of Purport
Off Calendar Sep-24-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: David Mattingly appearing for petitioners. Lawrence Lanctot and Mr. Stikkly appearing for respondent. Mr. Mattingly requested to have this matter set for trial. His estimate time for trial is five days. Mr. Lanctot's estimate time is two to three days. Matter is referred to the Presiding Judge to set for trial. Order signed.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Rob Harris.

Verified Petition To Determine Validity Of Purport
Off Calendar Aug-29-2007 Continued To Sep-24-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Respondents Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment And/Or Summary Adjudication;
Off Calendar Sep-13-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Lawrence Lanctot and Timothy Watson appearing for respondent/moving party David LeDuc. David Mattingly appearing for petitioner/opposing party. Mr. Mattingly wanted to make oral argument after seeing the tentative ruling. Court wanted him to concentrate on statue of limitation. Oral arguments presented by both sides on that issue. Issue on statue of limitation is submitted. Other issues including lack of testamentary capacity and undue influence argued. Both counsel asked for ruling on objections. After listening to the oral arguments, Court agreed to take a further look at 437C and the evidentary objections. The status hearing is coming up next week. Court asked for a little more time to take a further look. Counsel will call the clerk to continue the status hearing to second week of September. Per diem fee of $280.00 is charged. Both counsel will send in their share of the fee.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Dave Davenport CSR#1694. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: A. Aguerre.

Verified Petition To Determine Validity Of Purport
Off Calendar Jun-25-2007 Continued To Aug-29-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Verified Petition To Determine Validity Of Purport
Off Calendar Mar-05-2007 Continued To Jun-25-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Lawrence Lanctot appearing for respondent David LeDuc. Aaron Minnis appearing for petitioner. Mr. Minnis is asking to have this matter set for trial. Counsel can not agree on length of trial. Ms. Lanctot thinks it can be done in two 1/2 day and Mr. Minnis thinks it will take three full days. Mr. Lanctot is planning on filing some pretrial motions. Matter is continued to 6/25. Court will either set trial date here or refer the matter to the Presiding Judge for trial setting.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: J. Peralta.

Respondent Edgewood'S Motion To Quash Service For Lack Of Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Feb-05-2007 Written Order Received
MINI MINUTES: David Mattingly appearing for opposing party/petitioner. Robert Staley appearing for Edgewood, moving party/respondent.
Court's tentative ruling is Motion to Quash Service for Lack of Jurisdiction is granted. Oral argument presented by both counsel. The Court's tentative ruling stands. Edgewood is dismissed under 581. Mr. Staley will prepare an order.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Leticia Perez CSR# 7782. Clerk: Ella Yip.

Verified Petition To Determine Validity Of Purport
Off Calendar Jan-29-2007 Continued To Mar-05-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Third Amended Verified Petition
Off Calendar Jan-11-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No parties, counsel, or interested persons appeared on the matter.

The Court noted at the outset that the tentative ruling was to grant the Motion to Strike with leave to amend the Petition up to and including January 22, 2007. Respondent would have ten (10) days thereafter to file a responsive pleading. The Court further noted that no party had otherwise notified the Court that it would appear to contest the tentative ruling. There being no opposition to the tentative ruling, the tentative ruling was adopted as the ruling of the Court and the Order for same was signed in open court.

Judicial Office: Dorothy L. McMath, Commissioner; Reporter: Susan Lee CSR #4072; Bailiff: Deputy Mizota; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Investigator: None.

Verified Petition To Determine Validity Of Purport
Off Calendar Dec-05-2006 Continued To Dec-19-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petitioner'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Re: Deposition Of Catherine Owens
Off Calendar Dec-05-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Petitioner Mary Kriley and Catherine Owens' Motion for a protective order is granted in part and denied in part. The deposition of Catherine Owens shall resume but be limited in duration to no more than three hours on the record. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Verified Petition To Determine Validity Of Purport
Off Calendar Oct-17-2006 Continued To Dec-05-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

To Compel Deposition Testimony Of Petitioner Catherine M. Owens; For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions Against Petitioners And Their Counsel
Off Calendar Oct-13-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lawrence R. Lanctot for respondent and moving party: David LeDuc
Aaron P. Minnis for petitioner and opposing party: Mary K. Owens

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted with modification: Respondent David Le Duc's motion to compel the deposition of Catherine Owens and the production of documents is granted. Counsel for the parties are ordered to meet and confer as to a mutually convenient time to resume the deposition. The deposition shall be completed by November 13, 2006. The Court finds that counsel for petitioner terminated the deposition and opposed the instant motion without substantial justification.
Modification: Sanctions $884 and deposition to be completed by 12/13/06 unless motion for a protective order brought and granted. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Verified Petition
Off Calendar Oct-19-2006 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Lawrence Lanctot appearing for respondent David LeDuc. Aaron Minnis appearing for petitioners.
On Motion for leave to file third amended petition. Tentative ruling is Motion is granted. Oral argument presented by both counsel. Matter submitted. Court wants proposed orders from both counsel. Mr. Minnis presents his proposed order. Mr. Lanctot will submit his proposed order.
On Motion to appoint lawsuit representative. Tentative ruling is Motion is denied. CCP 395 does not say appoint a lawsuit representative. Oral argument presented by Mr. Minnis. Tentative ruling stands. Motion is denied. Mr. Lanctot will submit an order. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Susan Lee. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.
